name: FileCreateStreamHash
definition: Sysmon Event ID 15 records creation of a named file stream and hashes stream content.
trigger_scenarios: Microsoft documents that this event logs when a named file stream is created and records hashes of the unnamed file contents and the named stream, with Zone.Identifier as a common mark-of-the-web stream.

key_fields.explanation: The file and stream path. A colon suffix such as file.txt:payload.exe indicates an NTFS alternate data stream.
Sysmon records hashes of stream content, which should be used to preserve and triage the hidden payload.
The process that created the stream and the join key to Event ID 1 command-line context.

false_positives: Browsers create Zone.Identifier streams on downloaded files.
Backup and endpoint tools may inspect or create metadata streams during scanning.
detection_notes: Alert when TargetFilename contains an ADS suffix such as C:\Users\Public\invoice.txt:payload.exe or a non-Zone.Identifier stream under Downloads, Temp, or ProgramData. Microsoft documents Event 15 for named file streams and Zone.Identifier; MITRE T1564.004 covers Alternate Data Streams used to hide data. Correlate ProcessGuid to Event 1 and preserve the stream hash before deleting the host artifact.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 15
| where TargetFilename contains ":"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, TargetFilename, Hashes, ProcessGuid
spl_snippet: index=sysmon EventCode=15 TargetFilename="*:*" | table _time, host, Image, TargetFilename, Hashes, ProcessGuid
sample_log:
UtcTime: 2026-07-13 03:03:00.000
Image: C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe
TargetFilename: C:\Users\Public\invoice.txt:payload.exe
Hashes: SHA256=REDACTED
ProcessGuid: {REDACTED}

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 15
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 15
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields described on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 15
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=15
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Apply the documented field conditions after validating local field extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 15
assumptions: Confirm Provider is Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon because event IDs are not globally unique.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 15
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 15
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 15
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the event-specific conditions and correlations documented on this page.

value_references.meaning: A named file stream was created and its content was hashed.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret EventID with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: The browser Mark-of-the-Web alternate data stream documented by Sysinternals.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret TargetFilename with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 15 mean?
faqs.answer: A named file stream was created and Sysmon recorded hashes and stream content.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 15 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Browsers routinely create Zone.Identifier streams on downloaded files.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 15 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Distinguish Zone.Identifier from unusual stream names and correlate ProcessGuid with file creation and process execution.
